mechanism_narrative: A lower or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and equity financing costs, so when policy is already near the effective floor the S&P 500 should continue higher over the next 20 trading days as liquidity-sensitive valuation effects dominate.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling or at a local low, or if rolling 3-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings pressure, so once unemployment jumps above its recent trend the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both improve together, payroll gains and retail-sales growth indicate broad demand strength that supports revenues and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous weakening of INDPRO and HOUST marks a supply-side contraction in production and housing activity, which raises recession risk and lowers equity returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the drawdown profile is indistinguishable from random after transaction-cost adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy becomes more accommodative and inflation surprises are absorbed by easier financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce positive 5-day returns at least 60% of the time, or if higher CPI with low FEDFUNDS is followed by negative short-horizon equity reactions.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising UNRATE after an employment slowdown signals that earnings revisions will keep falling, so the S&P 500 should decline over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 8% and PAYEMS growth is negative over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-unemployment and declining-payroll regime is not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend indicates improving aggregate final demand and revenue capacity, which should lift equities over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP growth does not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns over walk-forward samples, or if the signal’s rank correlation remains below 0.02 for any two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and HOUST both rebound after weakness, the economy is transitioning from supply disruption to recovery, and equity returns should turn positive over the next 20 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns up after a 3-month decline and HOUST increases month over month
failure_condition: Reject if rebound episodes in INDPRO and HOUST are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is not significantly above zero.
